scene_description: 5-slide carousel: Slide 1 = glowing skin close-up selfie. Slide 2-4 = skincare products flat-lay, routine steps, 'before coffee' look. Slide 5 = radiant after-routine selfie.
on_screen_text:
Slide 1: 'My skincare philosophy is simple.'
Slide 2: 'Cleanse. Hydrate. Protect.'
Slide 3: 'Consistency beats expensive products.'
Slide 4: 'Glow from within first.'
Slide 5: 'This is my 5-minute morning ritual ✨'
caption:
I used to overcomplicate skincare.

Then I realised: consistency, hydration and SPF beat a 12-step routine every single time.

Now my skin has never felt better — and my routine takes less than 5 minutes.

💆‍♀️ What's one skincare step you swear by? Drop it below!
hook: Your skin is a reflection of how you treat yourself 🌿
cta: Drop your skincare holy grail product below 💧
location: Bathroom vanity or clean white desk
music_suggestion: Soft ASMR sounds or 'Daydream' – Bazzi
notes: Perfect even lighting — ring light or softbox. Close-up skin texture shots should be sharp. Products: clean aesthetic, grouped neatly. No harsh shadows.

scene_description: Day-in-the-life vlog-style reel. Morning coffee → outfit check → café work session → lunch outside → golden hour walk. Text timestamps at each segment.
on_screen_text:
'7am: coffee & quiet.
11am: work from café.
2pm: lunch al fresco.
6pm: golden hour walk.
Result: a really good day.'
caption:
Not every day is a travel adventure or a fashion shoot.

Some days are just… beautiful in the most ordinary way.

Coffee with no rush. Work you love. Fresh air. A sunset that reminds you how lucky you are.

🌸 What does your perfect day look like? Tell me below!
hook: Come spend a day with me ☀️ (it's a good one)
cta: Tell me what your perfect day looks like 👇
music_suggestion: Trending 'day in my life' audio or 'Golden Hour' – JVKE
notes: Continuous story-arc editing. Transitions between scenes should feel seamless. Add timestamps as text overlay. End with a peaceful closing smile-to-camera frame.

theme: Relationship Advice – Red Flags vs Green Flags
scene_description: Punchy text-card reel with aesthetic lifestyle B-roll in background (coffee, walking, flowers). Two-part format: Red flags → Green flags. Warm, direct tone.
on_screen_text:
Red flags ❌:
'Inconsistency. Excuses. Drama.'
Green flags 💚:
'Calm energy. Effort. Growth.'
caption:
Nobody talks enough about green flags.

We're all so trained to watch for red flags that we forget to notice when something is actually healthy and good.

Calm energy. Consistency. Someone who makes you better.

That's what a good relationship feels like.

💬 What's your biggest green flag? Comment below — let's build a list 👇
hook: Green flags I look for in a relationship 💚 (save this one)
cta: Comment your biggest green flag 💚👇
music_suggestion: 'Is It Too Much to Ask' – Adele or trending relationship advice audio
notes: Text cards: bold white font on semi-transparent overlay. Background B-roll should be warm and aesthetic, not distracting. 2-3 seconds per card. Satisfying pacing.

scene_description: 5-slide spring lookbook carousel. Outfit 1: floral midi dress. Outfit 2: linen co-ord set. Outfit 3: white sundress + straw hat. Outfit 4: pastel blazer + tailored shorts. Outfit 5: chic all-white look.
on_screen_text:
Slide 1: 'Spring lookbook — let's go.'
Slide 2: 'Floral always works.'
Slide 3: 'Linen = effortless.'
Slide 4: 'A hat is a whole personality.'
Slide 5: 'All white. All confidence.'
caption:
Spring is my favourite season to dress for — the colours, the fabrics, the energy.

I put together 5 looks that feel effortless but intentional.

All of these can be styled up or down depending on where you're headed.

🌸 Which look is your favourite? Comment the number below and save this for your next shopping haul!
hook: 5 spring outfits you need this season 🌸 (swipe all the way!)
cta: Comment your favourite look (1-5) & save this 💾
music_suggestion: 'Flowers' – Miley Cyrus or upbeat spring playlist
notes: Consistent backdrop per outfit OR matching colour mood across all slides. Use natural light. Each outfit should have a clear full-body + close-up detail shot.

theme: Personal Growth – Things I Learned in My 20s
scene_description: Aesthetic talking-head or text-card reel. Close-up of her face, confident expression, warm background. Quick-cut lessons as text overlays.
on_screen_text:
'Your 20s:
Protect your peace.
Travel more.
Stop comparing.
Trust yourself.
That's it.'
caption:
I'm still in my 20s and these lessons have already changed my entire life.

Nobody hands you a manual.
You learn by doing — and sometimes by getting it completely wrong.

But if I could tell my younger self anything, it's this.

💛 Save this and share it with a friend in their 20s who needs to hear it.
hook: Things I wish I knew at 20 (writing this so you don't have to figure it out alone) 💛
cta: Save & share with a friend who needs this 💛
music_suggestion: 'Vienna' – Billy Joel (acoustic) or motivational trending sound
notes: Warm candlelight or golden hour. Speak directly to camera with gentle but confident energy. Subtitle everything. End with a smiling freeze-frame.

theme: Lifestyle – How I Plan My Week
scene_description: Aesthetic productivity carousel. Slide 1 = weekly planner on desk. Slide 2 = morning ritual. Slide 3 = focus work session. Slide 4 = movement/walk. Slide 5 = reflection journal.
on_screen_text:
Slide 1: 'My week doesn't plan itself.'
Slide 2: 'Start with a non-negotiable morning.'
Slide 3: 'Deep work first, socials second.'
Slide 4: 'Move your body every single day.'
Slide 5: 'Sunday reset is sacred.'
caption:
I used to wing every week and wonder why I felt behind.

Now I do a simple Sunday planning session and everything flows better.

It's not about productivity hacks — it's about knowing your priorities and protecting time for what matters.

📋 Save this if you want to feel more in control of your week! What's your Sunday reset ritual?
hook: How I actually plan my week (the realistic version) 📋
cta: Save this & tell me your Sunday reset ritual 📋
music_suggestion: Lo-fi study beats or 'Work' – Charlotte OC
notes: Clean, minimal desk setup. Natural light. Planner/journal should be open and written in — not blank. Shows real use, not performance.

scene_description: Day-to-night outfit transformation reel. Daytime: linen set and trainers. Nighttime: slip dress, heels, gold jewellery. Quick transition in a mirror or wardrobe.
on_screen_text:
'Day:
Casual and comfortable.
Night:
Glam and unforgettable.
Same girl. Different energy.'
caption:
Because we all contain multitudes.

Mornings call for effortless ease. Evenings call for a little drama.

This is my favourite kind of outfit challenge — showing how versatile your wardrobe can actually be when you think creatively.

Drop a 🌙 if you're more of a night-out person!
hook: Day to night in 30 seconds — you asked for it 🌙✨
cta: Drop a 🌙 if you're a night-out person!
location: Mirror in stylish room or hotel hallway
music_suggestion: 'Levitating' – Dua Lipa remix or trending fashion transition audio
notes: Mirror transition must be perfectly synced with the beat drop. Film at the same position both times. The 'glam' look needs to be genuinely elevated — not just different.

scene_description: 5-frame interactive story. Mix of selfies, polls, question boxes and this-or-that slides.
on_screen_text:
Frame 1: 'Friday feels! How are you spending tonight? 🌙' (Poll: In/Out)
Frame 2: 'This or that: Bali 🌴 or Paris 🗼?'
Frame 3: 'Be honest — are you a morning or night person?' (Poll)
Frame 4: 'Ask me your most random question 👇' (Question box)
Frame 5: 'Your answers are making me smile 🤍 Keep them coming!'
caption: (Story format — engagement focused)
hook: Let's play a quick game 👀 (your answers will surprise you)
cta: Vote in the polls & drop your question below 👇
music_suggestion: Story background: 'good 4 u' – Olivia Rodrigo or fun upbeat audio
notes: Use brand colours. Mix font sizes for visual rhythm. Add animated GIF stickers for energy. Keep each frame fast and fun — 3-4 seconds each.

scene_description: Cinematic travel montage reel showing 5+ destinations. Each shot = 2-3 seconds. Landscapes, streets, food markets, sunsets. No talking — pure visual storytelling.
on_screen_text:
'Place 1: Bali — taught me to slow down.
Place 2: Paris — taught me beauty matters.
Place 3: Santorini — taught me stillness.
Place 4: New York — taught me ambition.
Place 5: Home — taught me gratitude.'
caption:
Some places change you.

Not because they're picture-perfect — but because they shift something inside you.

Every destination on this list taught me something I couldn't have learned anywhere else.

🌍 What place changed YOU? Drop it in the comments — I'm making a list for my next trip!
hook: 5 places that completely changed the way I see the world 🌍
cta: Comment the place that changed you 🌍👇
location: Bali / Paris / Santorini / New York / London
music_suggestion: 'Ode to the Mets' – The Strokes or cinematic travel trending audio
notes: Colour grade each destination differently to show contrast of place. Ratio 9:16 vertical. Each clip must be visually distinct. No shaky footage — use stabiliser.

theme: Relationship – 'My Honest Thoughts on Modern Dating'
scene_description: Bold opinion-style carousel. Slide 1 = elegant seated pose with direct eye contact. Slide 2-5 = statement quote cards on minimal background.
on_screen_text:
Slide 1: 'Let's talk about modern dating.'
Slide 2: 'We have endless options. And yet…'
Slide 3: 'We confuse communication with texting.'
Slide 4: 'We confuse chemistry with comfort.'
Slide 5: 'Know what you want. Say it. Then live by it.'
caption:
I've been thinking about this a lot lately.

We're more 'connected' than ever — and somehow lonelier than ever.

I think we've forgotten how to be patient. How to communicate openly. How to choose someone instead of collecting options.

Just a thought. Maybe I'm wrong.

💬 What do you think is the biggest problem with modern dating? I genuinely want to know.
hook: Unpopular opinion: modern dating is hard because we've forgotten what we actually want 💬
cta: Comment your honest take on modern dating 💬
location: Stylish indoor setting — dim warm lighting
music_suggestion: 'Someone Like You' – Adele (piano version) or emotional lo-fi
notes: Direct-to-camera gaze on slide 1 — confident, not confrontational. Quote slides: minimal, bold typography. No clutter. Let the words breathe.

scene_description: GRWM reel — no makeup to finished look. Quick voiceover or text overlay explaining each step. Warm vanity lighting. Confident, natural energy throughout.
on_screen_text:
'Step 1: SPF — always.
Step 2: Concealer only where needed.
Step 3: A lip that does the work.
Step 4: A spritz of confidence.
And you're done.'
caption:
My GRWM routine is short, intentional and built around enhancing what's already there — not covering it up.

I believe makeup should feel like self-expression, not a mask.

💄 What's the ONE product you can't leave the house without? Drop it below — I might be adding it to my routine!
hook: Get ready with me for a day out ✨ (honest skincare to glam)
cta: Tell me your one desert island beauty product 💄
music_suggestion: Upbeat pop or 'Confident' – Demi Lovato
notes: Ring light at eye level. Show the process — imperfection is relatable. Don't rush cuts. Each step = 3-5 seconds. Final look reveal in full-length mirror.

scene_description: Aesthetic motivational reel. Quick cuts between: blank journal → written journal, bare desk → styled workspace, door → open world. Metaphorical visual storytelling.
on_screen_text:
'You will never feel 100% ready.
Start anyway.
Clarity comes from doing,
not waiting.'
caption:
I waited so long to start this page because I wanted everything to be 'perfect'.

The lighting. The following. The content.

But perfectionism is just fear in a fancy outfit.

So I started. Imperfectly. And it's the best decision I've made.

🔑 What are YOU waiting to start? Say it out loud in the comments — accountability starts here.
hook: The secret nobody tells you about starting something new… 🔑
cta: Comment what you're waiting to start — say it out loud 🔑
music_suggestion: 'Eye of the Tiger' (lo-fi version) or trending motivational audio
notes: Metaphorical prop transitions — show the 'before' and 'after' of courage. Keep energy high but voice calm. Text reveals synced to beat.

scene_description: Weekly outfit summary reel. 5-7 outfits shown in fast, confident clips. Each clip = 3-5 seconds. Variety of settings — home, street, café, evening.
on_screen_text:
'Monday: 8/10 ✨
Tuesday: 9/10 🔥
Wednesday: 7/10 🤍
Thursday: 10/10 💛
Friday: debatable… 😂'
caption:
The weekly outfit roundup you didn't know you needed.

Some weeks I nail it. Some weeks I try. This was one of the nail-it weeks.

Fashion should be fun — not stressful. These looks were all built from pieces I already owned, which is honestly the best styling challenge.

👗 Rate your favourite look in the comments and tell me what you'd add!

cta: Rate your favourite look 1-10 in the comments 👗

notes: Consistent aspect ratio across all clips. Match outfit transitions to music beats. Show full-body shots — not just face. Represent her personal style fully.

scene_description: 5-slide self-care carousel. Slide 1 = bath with candles and rose petals. Slide 2 = skincare flat-lay. Slide 3 = reading in robe. Slide 4 = journaling. Slide 5 = cosy evening look.
on_screen_text:
Slide 1: 'Sunday is sacred.'
Slide 2: 'Nourish your skin.'
Slide 3: 'Feed your mind.'
Slide 4: 'Empty your thoughts onto a page.'
Slide 5: 'Then rest. Fully and unapologetically.'
caption:
I protect my Sundays with everything I have.

No errands. No hustle. No guilt.

Just a full day of filling back up so the rest of the week can flow.

Self-care isn't a treat. It's maintenance. It's how I show up for everyone and everything I love.

🕯️ What's your non-negotiable self-care Sunday ritual? Tell me below!
hook: Sunday is for the soul 🕯️ (my full self-care ritual inside)
cta: Tell me your non-negotiable Sunday ritual 🕯️

notes: Candlelight and warm lamp light only — no overhead lighting. Soft, dreamy aesthetic. Every prop should feel intentional. Bath bombs, real flowers, not fake.

theme: Viral – 'Tell Me You're Confident Without Telling Me' Challenge
scene_description: Quick-fire text-card reel with confident but humble statements. Aesthetic background B-roll of walking, coffee, travel shots. Punchy, fun energy.
on_screen_text:
'You walk into a room and don't scan for approval.
You speak your mind kindly.
You know your worth quietly.
You smile — because life is genuinely good.'
caption:
Confidence isn't loud. It's not arrogance.

It's knowing who you are, what you stand for and being completely comfortable in your own skin.

The most confident people I know are also the most humble.

💬 Tell me how YOU show confidence without saying it — I want to see your answers!
hook: Tell me you're confident without telling me you're confident 😏
cta: Comment your version of quiet confidence below 💬
music_suggestion: Trending viral challenge audio or 'Confident' – Demi Lovato
notes: Fast, punchy cuts. High energy editing. Text should 'pop' — bold white on slight shadow. End with a slow, self-assured close-up of her face.

scene_description: Travel wishlist carousel. 5 slides each featuring a different dream destination: Bali rice fields, Amalfi Coast, Kyoto temples, Maldives overwater bungalow, Morocco riad courtyard.
on_screen_text:
Slide 1: 'Where I'm going (in my mind for now 😂)'
Slide 2: 'Bali — for the soul.'
Slide 3: 'Amalfi — for the beauty.'
Slide 4: 'Kyoto — for the stillness.'
Slide 5: 'Maldives — because why not dream big.'
caption:
Vision boards are powerful.

I genuinely believe that consistently visualising where you want to go — mentally and physically — makes it happen faster.

Every destination on this list has been on my mind for months.

✈️ Tell me YOUR top dream destination and let's manifest it together. Mine might just become yours too.

cta: Drop your #1 dream destination below ✈️

notes: Use cinematic destination photography — not tourist photos. Rich colours. Each destination should feel distinct. Dreamy soft vignette on edges.

theme: Viral Growth – '30 Days Later: What I Learned'
scene_description: Reflection reel. Montage of her best moments from the past 30 days — outfit clips, travel shots, coffee moments, city walks. Ends with direct-to-camera gratitude.
on_screen_text:
'30 days ago I started.
I had no followers.
No perfect plan.
Just a feeling.
Now I know:
Every great journey starts
with one honest step.'
'Thank you for being here. 🤍'
caption:
30 days.

I started this page with no followers, no strategy and honestly — a little fear.

And what happened was more beautiful than I expected.

I found a community. I found my voice. I found that sharing your authentic self is the most powerful thing you can do.

Thank you for every comment, every like, every save. You make this real.

This is just the beginning. Stay with me 🌸

🌸 Drop a ❤️ if you've been here from the start — you're my favourite people.
hook: 30 days in — and I'm just getting started 🌸 (a message for you)
cta: Drop a ❤️ if you've been here from the start 🌸

notes: Best-of montage — use her strongest visual moments from all 30 days. End frame: warm direct smile to camera with 'thank you' text. Emotional, genuine, not over-edited.

theme: Engagement – 'I Bet You Can Relate' Reel
scene_description: Relatable lifestyle reel. Split-screen or text-card format showing 'what I planned vs what I actually did'. Funny but warm and self-aware tone.
on_screen_text:
'Planned: 6am workout.
Actual: 8:30am coffee scroll.

Planned: healthy lunch.
Actual: café croissant again.

Planned: early night.
Actual: one more episode.'

'And yet — somehow still thriving. 🌸'
caption:
Real life is never the aesthetic Instagram version.

And honestly? That's what makes it so relatable and so beautiful.

Between the planned workouts and unplanned croissants, we're all just figuring it out.

😂 Comment DONE if you related to at least one of these — I need to know I'm not alone!

notes: Keep it light and fun. Her self-awareness and humour are her charm here. Split-screen: 'expected vs reality'. Use her real energy — not scripted laughter.

scene_description: Flat lay or lifestyle photo: ceramic coffee cup, croissant, open book, small vase of flowers. Natural light from left. Warm beige tones.
on_screen_text:
'Good things take time.
So does a perfect morning.'
caption:
This is my non-negotiable.

Before emails, before notifications, before anything — I give myself this quiet hour.

Coffee. A page or two of a good book. Sunlight.

The rest of the day can wait.

🌸 Are you a morning person or night owl? Tell me in the comments!

This page started with inspiration.

So I decided to start my own journey.

This page will be about style, travel, growth and the little lessons life teaches us.

hook: The most important relationship you'll ever have is with yourself 💫
cta: Save this & share with a friend who needs it 💾
music_suggestion: 'All of Me' – John Legend (soft version) or piano lo-fi
notes: Deep jewel tones — burgundy, gold, emerald. Elegant but warm. Quote slides should use a classic serif font on a light textured background.

scene_description: Soft-focus portrait in a sunlit indoor setting — natural light, floral background, genuine smile, minimal jewellery, flowing midi dress.
on_screen_text:
This page started with inspiration.
Now it's becoming my story.
caption:
For a long time I admired creators who turn everyday moments into something beautiful.

So I decided to start my own journey.

This page will be about style, travel, growth and the little lessons life teaches us.

If that sounds like something you'd enjoy — stay with me 🤍

Where are you joining from? Drop your city below 👇
hook: Hi, I'm here — and I'm just getting started 🌸
cta: Follow along & drop your city below 👇
music_suggestion: Lo-fi acoustic intro / Conan Gray - 'Astronomy' (soft version)
notes: Warm tones, soft bokeh background. Genuine, not posed. Should feel like meeting a friend for the first time.

hook: The start of something new.

scene_description: Walking reel in a stylish city street. Outfit: chic monochrome set or trench coat + tailored trousers. Camera follows from behind, then spins to face. Confident stride.
on_screen_text:
'Style your
own story.'
'No one does
you like you.'
caption:
There's a certain confidence that comes when you walk out the door and feel completely yourself.

Today's look is effortless meets intentional — because that's exactly the energy I'm bringing this week.

🔖 Save this look for outfit inspo!
What's your go-to city walk style? Let me know below 👇
hook: That Monday energy when the outfit is giving everything 🔥
cta: Save this look & tell me your favourite style combo 👇
music_suggestion: 'Levitating' – Dua Lipa or confidence-walk trending audio
notes: Film in portrait mode. Slow-motion 'hair flip' or coat twirl moment. Bright, sharp image — no moody filter. Confidence is everything in body language.

scene_description: Outfit transition reel: starts with casual home look (oversized shirt, jeans) → transforms into a polished spring floral midi dress outdoors.
on_screen_text:
Style tip:
Confidence is your
best accessory.
caption:
I spent years experimenting before finding looks that truly feel like me.

And honestly… I'm still evolving.

Fashion should be fun, expressive and a reflection of who you're becoming — not who the world expects you to be 💫

Save this if you love style inspo! 👆
hook: Finding your style is really finding yourself ✨
cta: Save this for your next outfit inspo 💾
music_suggestion: Trending audio: 'Espresso' – Sabrina Carpenter or upbeat pop transition sound
notes: Slow-motion transition at the 'reveal' moment. Film outdoors, golden hour preferred. Avoid heavy filters — keep skin tones natural.

scene_description: Aesthetic motivational reel. Quick cuts between: blank journal → written journal, bare desk → styled workspace, door → open world. Metaphorical visual storytelling.
on_screen_text:
'You will never feel 100% ready.
Start anyway.
Clarity comes from doing,
not waiting.'
caption:
I waited so long to start this page because I wanted everything to be 'perfect'.

The lighting. The following. The content.

But perfectionism is just fear in a fancy outfit.

So I started. Imperfectly. And it's the best decision I've made.

🔑 What are YOU waiting to start? Say it out loud in the comments — accountability starts here.
hook: The secret nobody tells you about starting something new… 🔑
cta: Comment what you're waiting to start — say it out loud 🔑
music_suggestion: 'Eye of the Tiger' (lo-fi version) or trending motivational audio
notes: Metaphorical prop transitions — show the 'before' and 'after' of courage. Keep energy high but voice calm. Text reveals synced to beat.

scene_description: A curated visual story highlighting the intersection of 10th-century Chalukyan architecture and the serene, tropical landscape of Konaseema. The aesthetic focuses on stone textures, symmetry, and the soft March sunlight.
on_screen_text: Draksharamam: A Masterclass in Ancient Design
caption: Spending my March wandering through the 'Dakshina Kasi' of Andhra Pradesh. The Draksharamam Bhimeswara Swamy Temple is a design lover’s dream—where monolithic grandeur meets the lush, green soul of Konaseema. Every carving feels like a whisper from the 10th century. 🛕✨
hook: Is this India's most architecturally significant hidden gem?
cta: Which detail do you find more captivating: the massive stone pillars or the intricate ceiling carvings? Let me know in the comments!
location: Draksharamam Bhimeswara Swamy Temple, Konaseema Zilla, AP
music_suggestion: A serene, atmospheric Carnatic Flute melody with a modern lo-fi undertone.
notes: Color grade with warm, earthy tones to complement the grey stone. Luna should wear a minimalist linen saree in a soft pastel shade to contrast with the ancient textures.

slides.sceneDescription: A long-distance, wide-angle shot of Luna standing at the massive stone entrance of the temple. She looks small in comparison to the towering structure, emphasizing the scale.

slides.onScreenText: The Beauty is in the Details
slides.sceneDescription: A macro close-up shot of the intricate carvings on a specific temple pillar, focusing on the craftsmanship and weathered stone texture.
A side profile shot of Luna walking slowly through the pillared corridors (Prakaram), with beams of March sunlight cutting through the columns.
A medium shot of Luna looking out towards the lush green coconut groves of Konaseema from the temple perimeter, bridging the gap between architecture and nature.

scene_description: Emotional, cinematic reel. She writes in a journal, then looks up at camera. Text overlays appear one by one — hopeful, warm, forward-looking.
on_screen_text:
'Dear future me:
You made it.
All the uncertainty.
All the growth.
All the moments that felt too big.
You handled them beautifully.
Keep going. 🤍'
caption:
I wrote this on a night when I wasn't sure about anything.

And something shifted when I put it down on paper.

We're all in the middle of something. None of us have it perfectly figured out.

But if we keep choosing growth, kindness and courage — we'll look back one day and know it was all worth it.

🤍 Write a letter to your future self in the comments — even just one line. I'll be reading every single one.
hook: A letter to my future self… 📝 (I hope she's proud)
cta: Write one line to your future self in the comments 📝
music_suggestion: 'The Night Will Always Win' – Manchester Orchestra or 'Fix You' piano version
notes: Slow, intentional editing. Candlelight. She should look genuinely reflective — not posed. Text appears slowly, word by word. Emotional pacing throughout.

scene_description: A visually cohesive series of shots capturing Sofia Laurant exploring the San Antonio River Walk, emphasizing the intersection of historic stone architecture and lush March greenery.
on_screen_text: A Morning on the River Walk
caption: There is something about the way the March sun hits the limestone here. San Antonio’s River Walk is more than just a destination; it’s a masterclass in urban landscape design. Spent my morning getting lost in the winding paths and early spring blooms. 🌿✨ The textures of the city are calling today.
hook: Escaping into the hidden design details of San Antonio.
cta: Which city has your favorite river walk architecture? Let’s chat in the comments!
music_suggestion: Acoustic, upbeat indie-folk or soft lo-fi jazz with nature sounds
notes: Focus on warm, natural lighting and the contrast between the turquoise water and the terracotta/limestone architecture. Ensure the outfit is a soft pastel to match the March spring palette.

slides.onScreenText: San Antonio in March hits different.
slides.sceneDescription: Long-distance view from a bridge. Luna is walking away along the riverside path, framed by overhanging cypress trees and stone arches.

slides.onScreenText: Finding design inspiration in every corner.
slides.sceneDescription: Medium side-profile shot of Luna leaning against a wrought-iron railing, looking out at a passing colorful river boat.
Close-up headshot of Luna smiling, with soft-focus spring flowers in the background and warm sunlight on her face.
Detail shot of Luna's hand touching the historic limestone walls or a shot of her stylish shoes stepping on the cobblestone path.

scene_description: Sofia Laurant walking across the iconic Brooklyn Bridge in a tailored, high-end black leather trench coat and boots. The atmosphere is crisp and overcast, typical of a chilly March day in New York, highlighting the architectural geometry of the bridge cables against her sleek outfit.
on_screen_text: March in Manhattan: 42 degrees and sharp.
caption: Bridging the gap between structural design and street style. New York in March demands layers that speak as loud as the skyline. There is something about the industrial steel of the Brooklyn Bridge that makes luxury leather feel right at home. Chilly, but worth every step.
hook: POV: You’re romanticizing a cold morning in Brooklyn.
cta: Which is your favorite NYC landmark for design inspiration?
music_suggestion: A cinematic, low-tempo jazz-hop beat with city sound overlays.
notes: Color grade with cool tones to emphasize the March chill and the rich texture of the leather.

slides.sceneDescription: A long-distance wide shot of Luna standing centered on the wooden planks of the bridge, cables framing her, looking toward the Manhattan skyline.
A side profile shot of Luna mid-stride, showing the movement of the leather trench coat and the texture of her gloves as she brushes hair from her face.
A close-up headshot/portrait focusing on her face and the high collar of the leather coat, with the blurred bridge cables in the background.
A low-angle detail shot of her luxury leather boots walking on the bridge's iconic wooden slats, focusing on the craftsmanship of the footwear.

scene_description: 5-frame story sequence: Frame 1 = close-up selfie. Frame 2 = question sticker. Frame 3 = poll. Frame 4 = fun 'this or that'. Frame 5 = thank-you note graphic.
on_screen_text:
Frame 1: 'Hi! I'm so happy you're here 🌸'
Frame 2: 'Ask me anything!' (question box)
Frame 3: Poll — 'What do you want more of?' Fashion / Travel
Frame 4: 'This or that: Coffee ☕ or Matcha 🍵?'
Frame 5: 'Thank you for day 1. This is just the beginning 🤍'
caption: (Story format — no long caption needed)
hook: Let's get to know each other — ask me anything! 🙋‍♀️
cta: Reply to the question box — I read every one 💌
music_suggestion: Story background music: 'good 4 u' – Olivia Rodrigo (upbeat, fun)
notes: Use brand colours as story backgrounds — light pink and white. Add GIF stickers for energy. Keep text large and readable.

scene_description: Calm, reflective talking-head style reel. Seated on a balcony or near a large window, holding a mug, soft background blur.
on_screen_text:
'Protect your peace.'
'Not everyone deserves
your energy.'
'And that's okay.'
caption:
The older I get, the more I value calm energy, genuine people and quiet mornings.

You don't have to respond to everything. You don't have to explain yourself to everyone.

Choose your circle wisely. Guard your joy.

Drop a ❤️ if you needed this reminder today.
hook: Nobody tells you this about protecting your peace… 👀
cta: Drop a ❤️ if this resonated with you
music_suggestion: 'Yellow' – Coldplay (acoustic) or soft R&B lo-fi
notes: No jump cuts — keep it calm and flowing. Speak slowly. Subtitle every word. Soft natural background — no distractions.
